svg.text: 40-pin GPIO header wiring — KY-022 IR receiver + 5 V cooling fan (PWM-controlled by CPU temp)
Radxa Dragon Q6A — 40-pin GPIO header (board rotated 90° CW · pin 1 at bottom-left)
software PWM ≈100 Hz, duty ∝ max CPU temp
to IR module S (yellow wire) · active-low
Waveshare RS485 CAN HAT-B uses pins 11/12/19/21–24/35–38/40.
Fan (4 / 6 / 8) and IR (1 / 9 / 15) are clear of the HAT footprint.
VS1838B open-collector, idle HIGH; tie S directly to GPIO_1 — no level shifter.
PWM drives the fan tach/control input (or an external MOSFET on 2-wire fans).
Linux side — DT overlays · fan-control daemon · IR keymap
NEC scancodes · Power → KEY_PROG1 (XF86Launch1) → GNOME launches Playbill
&tlmm gpio22 released from QUP6 UART, muxed as plain GPIO output (PIN_8)
reads max(cpu*-thermal.temp) → software PWM ≈100 Hz on gpio22
⚠ Pin 8 (gpio22) used to be the ttyMSM0 debug UART_TX. The serial console is dropped from the kernel cmdline; debug now uses tty1 + SSH only.
Playbill — GPIO peripheral wiring reference (IR + cooling fan)
